findings.description: Header layout differs - PDF has right-aligned official names/titles, HTML stacks them left-aligned below the logo
findings.evidence: PDF: PAT MCCRORY, Governor, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART, Secretary, S. JAY ZIMMERMAN, Director are right-aligned. HTML: These are left-aligned below the logo.

findings.description: The MEMORANDUM heading in PDF is centered and underlined, in HTML it is left-aligned and underlined with a colored rule below
findings.evidence: PDF: centered underlined 'MEMORANDUM'. HTML: left-aligned with underline and colored horizontal rule.

findings.description: Footer information appears between pages 1 and 2 in HTML rather than at the bottom of page 1 only
findings.evidence: PDF: footer with State of North Carolina contact info at bottom of page 1. HTML: same info appears as a separator between page content.

findings.description: Section headings I. History/Background style differs slightly - PDF uses bold with Roman numeral tab-indented format, HTML uses larger heading style
findings.evidence: PDF: 'I. History/Background' in bold with indentation. HTML: rendered as a larger heading style.

findings.description: The document has nested <main> elements. The outer <main> is from the template and the inner <main> is from the converted content, creating invalid HTML structure. However, the heading hierarchy itself (h1 for MEMORANDUM, h2 for sections I-IV) is reasonable.

The footer content (State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Resources, address, phone) appears both in the page header area and in a page-footer div within the body. This is acceptable as it mirrors the PDF's repeated footer.
findings.evidence: Footer text appears in the HTML header block and also in a page-footer div after the outline.

findings.description: The second image has empty alt text, which is acceptable for decorative images but the image appears to be a decorative line/bar at the footer.
findings.evidence: Second img tag: alt="" - likely a decorative footer bar, so empty alt is appropriate.
